title: Unsloth: Fast LLM Fine-tuning with 2x Speed Boost

segments.sentence: Unsloth is an open-source library that enables training and running open-source LLMs like Qwen, DeepSeek, gpt-oss, and Gemma locally with up to 2x faster performance and 70% less VRAM usage.

keyContent.concept: Unsloth is an open-source LLM fine-tuning library that dramatically accelerates training speed while reducing memory consumption.

segments.sentence: Unsloth supports over 500 models including Llama, Mistral, Gemma, Qwen, DeepSeek, gpt-oss, and handles text, vision, audio, and embedding models with optimized kernels.

keyContent.concept: Extensive model support covering major open-source architectures for diverse AI tasks.

segments.sentence: The library offers multiple training modes including 4-bit and 16-bit LoRA fine-tuning, full fine-tuning, pretraining, and FP8 training to balance speed and accuracy.

keyContent.concept: Flexible training options from quantized LoRA to full precision fine-tuning.

segments.sentence: Unsloth Studio provides a web-based user interface for running and training models on Windows, Linux, macOS, and WSL with real-time observability for monitoring loss and GPU usage.

keyContent.concept: No-code web UI making LLM training accessible without command-line expertise.

segments.sentence: The platform includes Data Recipes feature that auto-creates training datasets from PDFs, CSVs, and JSON documents through a visual node-based workflow editor.

keyContent.concept: Automated dataset preparation from unstructured documents for training.

segments.sentence: Unsloth provides the most memory-efficient reinforcement learning implementation, using 80% less VRAM for GRPO training and supporting FP8 reinforcement learning on consumer GPUs.

keyContent.concept: Advanced reinforcement learning capabilities with extreme memory optimization.

segments.sentence: Users can train models for free using Google Colab or Kaggle notebooks, with examples for Llama 3.1, gpt-oss, Qwen3, Gemma 3, and embedding models.

keyContent.concept: Free cloud notebook access for hands-on model training without local hardware.

segments.sentence: Models can be exported to GGUF, 16-bit safetensors, and other formats compatible with llama.cpp, vLLM, and Ollama for efficient inference deployment.

keyContent.concept: Flexible model export for deployment across various inference frameworks.

segments.sentence: Unsloth supports multi-GPU training and is compatible with NVIDIA RTX 30/40/50 series, Blackwell GPUs, AMD, and Intel accelerators with platform-specific installation guides.

keyContent.concept: Cross-platform GPU support including latest hardware architectures.

segments.sentence: Installation is available via pip, Docker containers, or nightly builds, with quickstart guides for Linux, Windows, macOS, and WSL environments using uv package manager.

keyContent.concept: Multiple installation methods for diverse development environments.
